description_long: This is a tall, rectangular lighthouse constructed primarily from white-painted masonry or concrete, featuring a robust and utilitarian design. The structure rises from a stone-paved base with a set of stairs leading up to a single entry door, flanked by low garden beds and surrounded by a grassy area. The tower is segmented into two main sections, with the upper portion slightly narrower and topped by a flat roof. A prominent gallery balcony with a metal railing encircles the upper section, providing access around the tower just below the lantern area. The windows and doors are simple and functional, with minimal decorative elements, emphasizing practicality over ornamentation. The overall appearance suggests a historical or early 20th-century functionalist architectural style, likely serving as a navigational aid in an urban or coastal park setting.
description: A tall, white, rectangular lighthouse with a gallery balcony and flat roof, featuring a stone base and entry stairs. Its design is utilitarian and functional, with minimal ornamentation.
description_view_0: Front view shows the main entrance with stairs leading up to a door, flanked by garden beds and a paved path.
description_view_1: Side view highlights the long staircase and the full height of the tower, with the gallery balcony and flat roof visible.
description_view_2: Rear view reveals a plain wall with some weathering, a paved area, and the absence of doors or windows on this side.
description_view_3: Opposite side view displays the secondary entrance or service door, the gallery balcony, and the surrounding landscaping.
